verses.text: As dead flies spoil the ointment, so 'a little folly' outweighs wisdom and honour; the wise heart inclines right, the fool's left. Small follies do great damage. A reputation built slowly can be ruined by one foolish act.

As dead flies spoil the ointment, so 'a little folly' outweighs wisdom and honour; the wise heart inclines right, the fool's left. Small follies do great damage. A reputation built slowly can be ruined by one foolish act.

As dead flies spoil the ointment, so 'a little folly' outweighs wisdom and honour; the wise heart inclines right, the fool's left. Small follies do great damage. A reputation built slowly can be ruined by one foolish act.

If a ruler's anger rises, yielding pacifies great offences; Solomon saw folly set on high and the worthy brought low. The world is often upside down. Wisdom keeps its place and waits, for misplaced honour does not last.

He that digs a pit may fall in it, and he that breaks a hedge be bitten; toil without wisdom wearies, but 'wisdom is profitable to direct.' Actions carry risk. Skill and forethought spare a man needless harm.

The words of the wise are gracious, but a fool's lips swallow himself; he multiplies words though no man knows the future. Speech reveals the heart. The fool talks on, while the wise weigh what they say.

The foolish wear themselves out, not knowing the way to the city; woe to a land whose king is a child, but blessed when its rulers are noble and temperate. Leadership shapes a nation. A land is happy when wisdom, not folly, governs it.

Slothfulness lets the building decay; a feast is for mirth and money answers many things; but curse not the king even in thought, for a bird may carry the matter. Guard your tongue and even your private words. Discretion is wise in a watching world.
